description_long: This is a vintage-style gramophone record player with a large, flared brass horn mounted on a wooden base. The base is rectangular and features decorative yellow floral motifs on its front and sides, with visible wear and chipped paint adding to its antique character. The turntable platter is metallic and circular, positioned centrally on the top surface, and accompanied by a prominent, curved metal tonearm. A hand-crank handle extends from the side, indicating manual operation. The horn is a dull gold color with visible seams and a slightly weathered finish, attached to the base by a silver metal support. The overall design is ornate and nostalgic, evoking early 20th-century audio equipment often found in urban antique shops or as decorative pieces in public spaces.
description: A vintage gramophone record player with a large brass horn, wooden base, and decorative yellow floral motifs. Features a metal turntable platter, curved tonearm, and hand-crank handle.
description_view_0: The record player is seen from a front angle, highlighting the large brass horn, wooden base with yellow floral motifs, and the metal turntable platter.
description_view_1: A direct front view shows the full opening of the horn, the circular platter, and the hand-crank handle extending to the right.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the back of the horn, the wooden base, and the handle extending directly toward the viewer.
description_view_3: A view from the opposite side, showing the horn's rear, the tonearm, and the hand-crank handle extending to the left.
attribute_turntable_platter: The turntable platter is present, circular, and metallic, occupying a central position on the wooden base. It is silver-gray in color, with a smooth, slightly worn finish and no visible grooves or patterns. Its size is proportionate to the base, covering about one-third of the top surface.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, made of metal, and has a curved shape. It is attached to the right side of the platter and extends over it, with a jointed appearance. The finish is matte silver, and it appears to be resting near the platter.
